824	/-- Compatibility of `finSuccEquiv` with the constant-variable inclusion
825	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the
826	coefficient inclusion `Polynomial.C`. Project-local because Mathlib only records the
827	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
828	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
829	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
830	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by
831	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
832	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl
833	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
834	  | mul_X p i hp =>
835	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hp, MvPolynomial.rename_X, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_X_succ,
836	      Polynomial.C_mul]
837	
838	/-- **L5b.3 — single-variable elimination engine** (shared)
839	(`lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic`, Nitsure §4). Let `R` be a commutative
840	ring and `p ∈ R[X_0,…,X_n]` a polynomial whose image under `finSuccEquiv` (a
841	univariate polynomial in `X_0` over `S := R[X_1,…,X_n]`) has a *unit* leading
842	coefficient — i.e. `p` is monic in `X_0` up to a unit. Then `R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)` is
843	module-finite over `S = R[X_1,…,X_n]`, the structure being via the constant
844	inclusion `S ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n]` (`rename Fin.succ`) followed by `Ideal.Quotient.mk`.
845	
846	Encoded as `RingHom.Finite` of that composite ring map (which unfolds to
847	`Module.Finite S (R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p))` with the exotic algebra structure) to avoid an
848	in-statement `letI` whose `Module` synthesis loops. The proof rescales `finSuccEquiv p`
849	by the inverse of its unit leading coefficient to a monic polynomial generating the
850	same ideal, invokes `Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient`, and transports along the
851	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
852	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
853	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
854	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
855	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
856	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
857	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
858	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
859	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
860	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
861	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
862	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
863	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
864	  have hcP :
865	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
866	    hc.map Polynomial.C
867	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
868	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
869	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
870	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
871	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
872	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
873	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
874	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)
875	  have hfinstd : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
876	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) := by
877	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient
878	  have hJ : Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
879	      = Ideal.map ((MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv :
880	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
881	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by
882	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
883	  let ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
884	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
885	    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {p}) (Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
886	      (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv hJ
887	  have hsmul : ∀ s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R,
888	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
889	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
890	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
891	          (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
892	          s := by
893	    intro s
894	    change ψ (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s))
895	      = Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C s)
896	    rw [Ideal.quotientEquiv_mk]
897	    exact congrArg _ (finSuccEquiv_rename_succ R n s)
898	  let Ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
899	      ≃ₐ[MvPolynomial (Fin n) R]
900	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
901	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv hsmul
902	  exact @Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
903	    (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
904	    (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
905	    _ _ _ _ algI.toModule hfinstd Ψ.symm.toLinearEquiv

907	/-- **L5b — torsion reindex onto fewer variables** (`lem:gf_torsion_reindex`,
908	Nitsure §4 support-dimension drop). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 1`, and `T`
909	a finite torsion module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `g ≠ 0` in `A` and
910	`m' < d` such that, after inverting `g`, the localisation `T_g` is module-finite
911	over `A_g[X_1,…,X_{m'}]` (one may take `m' = d - 1`). -/
912	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
913	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
914	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
915	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
916	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
917	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
918	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
919	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
920	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
921	      (_ : Module (Localization.Away g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
922	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
923	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
924	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
925	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
926	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) := by
927	  -- Reshape `d = m + 1` (uses `hd : 0 < d`); the distinguished eliminated variable is `X₀`.
928	  obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hd.ne'
929	  -- L5b.1 (`gf_torsion_annihilator`): a non-zero annihilator `F` of the torsion module `T`.
930	  obtain ⟨F, hF0, hFann⟩ := gf_torsion_annihilator A (m + 1) T htors
931	  -- L5b.2 (`gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`): a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a
932	  -- non-zero `g ∈ A` making `e F` monic in `X₀` up to a unit after inverting `g`.
933	  obtain ⟨g, hg0, e, hunit⟩ := gf_nagata_monic_lastVar A m F hF0
934	  -- L5b.3 (`mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar`): the coefficient subring
935	  -- `A_g[X₁,…,X_m] = MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g` has `A_g[X₀,…,X_m]/(map (e F))` module-finite
936	  -- over it; this is the source of the dimension drop `m' = m < m + 1`.
937	  have hfin := mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar (Localization.Away g) m
938	    (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F)) hunit
939	  -- REMAINING ASSEMBLY (localization-module transport plumbing, not a math blocker — see
940	  -- `task_results` for the precise chain): build a `P_g := MvPolynomial (Fin (m+1)) A_g`-module
941	  -- structure on `T_g := LocalizedModule (powers g) T` via the twist `T_e := compHom T e.symm`
942	  -- (so that `e F ∈ Ann(T_e)`, since `e.symm (e F) = F`), localize the quotient action
943	  -- `Module (P/(e F)) T_e` along `MvPolynomial.isLocalization` to get `Module (P_g/(G)) T_g`
944	  -- with `G := map (algebraMap A A_g) (e F)`, transport `hfin` across the
945	  -- localization-commutes-with-quotient iso `(P/(e F))_g ≅ P_g/(G)`, and conclude
946	  -- `Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g) T_g` by `Module.Finite.trans` through `P_g/(G)`.
947	  -- The witnesses are `g`, `m' := m` (with `Nat.lt_succ_self m`), the canonical A_g-module
948	  -- structure on `T_g`, and the (twist-derived) `MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g`-module + tower.
949	  sorry
950	
951	/-- **L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness** (`lem:gf_polynomial_core`,
952	Nitsure §4, the genuine Mathlib-absent residue). For `A` a noetherian domain and
953	`d ≥ 0`, a finite module `N` over the polynomial ring `A[X_1,…,X_d]`, regarded as
954	an `A`-module via the scalar tower `A → A[X] → N`, becomes free after inverting a
955	single non-zero `f ∈ A`.
956	
957	This is the bottom of the Nitsure §4 induction. The base case `d = 0` is the
958	finite-`A`-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`
959	(`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`). The inductive step builds the generic-rank short
960	exact sequence `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0` with `T` torsion of support
961	dimension `< d`, applies the inductive hypothesis to `T` and the torsion base
962	case `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion`, and splices via
963	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact`. -/
964	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial
965	    (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
966	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
967	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
968	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
969	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
970	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
971	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
972	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
973	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
974	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
975	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
976	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
977	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
978	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
979	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
980	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
981	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
982	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
983	  | _ d IH =>
984	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
985	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
986	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
987	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
988	      subst hd
989	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
990	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
991	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
992	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
993	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
994	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
995	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
996	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
997	        -- ring of definition.
998	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
999	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
1000	        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
1001	        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
1002	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1003	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1004	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1005	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1006	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1007	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1008	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1009	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1010	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1011	        --
1012	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1013	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1014	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1015	        --
1016	        -- The generic-rank SES is now concretely available from `gf_generic_rank_ses`:
1017	        obtain ⟨_mrank, _φ, _hφinj, _hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
1018	        -- `φ : (Fin mrank → P_d) →ₗ[P_d] N` is injective with torsion cokernel
1019	        -- `T := N ⧸ range φ` (`hTtors`). The remaining residue, to be assembled once
1020	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` (L5b, still `sorry`) lands, is:
1021	        --   1. equip `T` with its restricted `A`-module structure + scalar tower
1022	        --      (the restriction-of-scalars motive plumbing) and `Module.Finite P_d T`;
1023	        --   2. `gf_torsion_reindex A d hd T hTtors` ⟹ `g ≠ 0`, `m' < d`, and `T_g`
1024	        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_1,…,X_{m'}]`;
1025	        --   3. `IH m' hm' (Localization.Away g) T_g` ⟹ `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(T_g)_h`
1026	        --      free over `(A_g)_h` (the IH is now available AT BASE `A_g`);
1027	        --   4. descend the witness `h = a/g^k` to `f := g·a ∈ A` via
1028	        --      `free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul` (L3b);
1029	        --   5. the free left end `P_d^{⊕mrank}` localises to a free `A_f`-module, and
1030	        --      `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices the localised
1031	        --      SES `0 → (P_d^{⊕mrank})_f → N_f → T_f → 0` to give `N_f` free over `A_f`.
1032	        -- Steps 1–5 are blocked only on `gf_torsion_reindex`; the SES (step 0) and the
1033	        -- base-domain-generalised IH are both in hand.
1034	        sorry
1035	
1036	end GenericFreeness

1038	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1039	
1040	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1041	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1042	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1043	
1044	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
1045	
1046	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1047	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1048	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1049	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1050	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1051	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1052

1	\chapter{Flattening Stratification of a Coherent Sheaf}
2	\label{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4
6	% \emph{generic flatness} theorem: over a noetherian integral base, a coherent
7	% sheaf on a finite-type \(X \to S\) becomes flat over a non-empty open
8	% subscheme of \(S\). Both its algebraic form (generic freeness of a finite
9	% module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain) and its
10	% geometric form are recorded here as the prover-ready specification for
11	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.
12	%
13	% This chapter is independently parallel-startable: it does not gate on the
14	% Route~A.1.a relative-Spec chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.
15	
16	\section{Setup and motivation}
17	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
18	
19	Generic flatness is the foundational input to the flattening-stratification
20	construction underlying Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Quot scheme
21	(\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S4--\S5).  Its content is that flatness of a
22	coherent sheaf is a generic property over an integral base: over a noetherian
23	integral scheme \(S\), a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type morphism
24	\(X \to S\) becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open subscheme of
25	\(S\). Algebraically this is the statement that a finite module over a
26	finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain becomes free after inverting a
27	single non-zero element of the base ring.
28	
29	We work throughout with a noetherian (integral, where required) base scheme
30	\(S\), a finite-type morphism \(p : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
31	\(\F\).
32	
33	\section{Generic flatness}
34	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
35	
36	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
37	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
38	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
39	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
40	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
41	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
42	non-zero element of the base ring.
43	
44	\begin{theorem}
45	
46	
47	\leanok
48	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
49	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
50	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
51	  \uses{lem:gf_torsion_base, lem:noeth_prime_filtration, lem:gf_splice_shortExact,
52	        lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators, lem:gf_polynomial_core, lem:gf_finite_module}
53	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
54	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
55	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
56	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
57	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
58	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
59	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
60	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
61	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
62	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
63	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
64	
65	  The formal target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatnessAlgebraic} regards \(M\) as an
66	  \(A\)-module through the scalar tower \(A \to B \to M\), and packages the
67	  localisation \(M_f\) as the localised module at the powers of \(f\): there exists
68	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \texttt{LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M}
69	  is free over \texttt{Localization.Away f}.
70	\end{theorem}
71	
72	% INTENDED LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold pass creates the decl pinned above with
73	% exactly this signature):
74	%   (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
75	%     [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
76	%     [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
77	%     [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
78	%     ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
79	%       Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)
80	
81	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Over the quotient field \(K\) of \(A\), the \(K\)-algebra
82	% \(B_K = K\otimes _AB\) is of finite type, and \(M_K = K\otimes_AM\) is a
83	% finite module over \(B_K\). Let \(n\) be the dimension of the support
84	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
85	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]
86	% Now let \(n\ge 0\), and let the lemma be proved for smaller values.
87	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
88	% there exists a finite filtration
89	% \(0=M_0 \subset \ldots  \subset M_r =M\)
90	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
91	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
92	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
93	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
94	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
95	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
96	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
97	\begin{proof}
98	
99	  \textbf{Primary route — the finite \(A\)-module case as a thin Mathlib wrapper.}
100	  Suppose first that \(M\) is module-finite over \(A\) itself (i.e.\ \(M\) is a finite
101	  \(A\)-module, not merely a finite \(B\)-module). Since \(A\) is noetherian, a finite
102	  \(A\)-module is finitely presented over \(A\). Let \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) be the
103	  localisation of \(A\) at the multiplicative set \(S = A \setminus \{0\}\). The
104	  generic fibre \(M_K = K \otimes_A M = S^{-1} M\) is a \emph{finite-dimensional}
105	  \(K\)-vector space, hence free over \(K\); thus the freeness hypothesis at the
106	  generic localisation is automatic, with no input from the structure of \(B\). The
107	  result is then exactly Mathlib's
108	  \texttt{Module.FinitePresentation.exists\_free\_localizedModule\_powers}
109	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Free}): a finitely-presented \(A\)-module
110	  that is free over the localisation \(A_S = K\) becomes free over
111	  \texttt{Localization.Away}~\(r\) after inverting some \(r \in S\), i.e.\ some
112	  \(r \in A\), \(r \ne 0\). Setting \(f := r\) gives \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\). (The
113	  openness of the free locus is the \texttt{Module.freeLocus} API of
114	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.FreeLocus}, which records that the locus
115	  where \(M\) is free over \(A\) is open and, by the above, non-empty.) This closes
116	  the theorem whenever \(M\) is finite over \(A\).
117	
118	  \textbf{Surviving residue — reducing the finite-type \(B\)-algebra case to the
119	  finite \(A\)-module case.} The genuine content not supplied by Mathlib is the
120	  reduction of the general hypothesis (\(M\) finite over a \emph{finite-type}
121	  \(A\)-algebra \(B\)) to the finite \(A\)-module situation handled above. Concretely,
122	  the missing piece is the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness: a finite module
123	  over \(A[b_1, \dots, b_n]\) becomes free over \(A_f\) after inverting a single
124	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\). The classical \S4 dévissage below is the fallback route
125	  for exactly this residue.
126	
127	  \emph{Fallback (Nitsure \S4 induction), as a \texttt{\textbackslash uses}-linked chain.}
128	  The \S4 dévissage is assembled from the sub-lemmas of
129	  \cref{sec:gf_devissage_chain}. If \(M_K = 0\), the torsion base case
130	  \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} gives \(f \ne 0\) with \(M_f = 0\) free. Otherwise the
131	  prime-filtration induction principle \cref{lem:noeth_prime_filtration} over the
132	  noetherian ring \(B\), combined with the splicing fact
133	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact}, reduces to \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) and hence to
134	  \(B\) a domain, \(M = B\); Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators
135	  \cref{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators} makes \(B_g\) finite over a polynomial
136	  ring \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), and the polynomial-ring core
137	  \cref{lem:gf_polynomial_core} — bottoming out at the finite-\(A\)-module leaf
138	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module} of the primary route — produces the required
139	  \(f \ne 0\) with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\).
140	\end{proof}
141	
142	\subsection{Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue}
143	\label{sec:gf_devissage_chain}
144	
145	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
146	\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} — the case where \(M\) is finite over the
147	finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\) but not module-finite over \(A\) — into the
148	classical \cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}~\S4 induction. They are ordered so that each
149	\texttt{\textbackslash uses} only earlier blocks (or Mathlib anchors). Throughout,
150	\(A\) is a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\), \(B\) a
151	finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module with the compatible
152	\(A\)-module structure via the scalar tower \(A \to B \to M\).
153	
154	\begin{lemma}\leanok
155	[Finite-module leaf — finite \(A\)-module case]
156	  \label{lem:gf_finite_module}
157	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite}
158	  \uses{lem:fp_free_descent}
159	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(M\) a finite \(A\)-module. Then there
160	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
161	\end{lemma}
162	
163	\begin{proof}
164	
165	  This is the \(d = 0\) leaf of the induction and is already closed in Lean
166	  axiom-clean. A finite module over a noetherian ring is finitely presented, so
167	  \(M\) is finitely presented over \(A\). Over the generic point
168	  \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A) = S^{-1}A\) (\(S = A \setminus \{0\}\)) the localisation
169	  \(M_K = S^{-1}M\) is a finite-dimensional \(K\)-vector space, hence free over
170	  \(K\). The finite-presentation free-descent lemma \cref{lem:fp_free_descent}
171	  then descends this freeness to a single basic open: there is \(r \in S\), i.e.\
172	  \(r \in A\) with \(r \ne 0\), such that \(M_r\) is free over \(A_r\). Take
173	  \(f := r\).
174	\end{proof}
175	
176	\begin{lemma}[Finite-presentation free descent]
177	  \label{lem:fp_free_descent}
178	  \lean{Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers}
179	  \mathlibok
180	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
181	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative subset, and
182	  \(M\) a finitely-presented \(R\)-module such that the localisation \(M_S\) is
183	  free over the localisation \(R_S\). Then there exists \(r \in S\) such that
184	  \(M_r\) is free over \(R_r = \texttt{Localization.Away}~r\) (of the same finite
185	  rank).
186	\end{lemma}
187	
188	\begin{lemma}\leanok
189	[L1 — torsion base case \(M_K = 0\)]
190	  \label{lem:gf_torsion_base}
191	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion}
192	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, base case
193	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1726).
194	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
195	  % "We argue by induction on $n$, starting with $n=-1$ which is the case when
196	  %  $M_K =0$. In this case, as $K\otimes _AM = S^{-1}M$ where $S = A - \{ 0\}$,
197	  %  each $v\in M$ is annihilated by some non-zero element of $A$. Taking a finite
198	  %  generating set, and a common multiple of corresponding annihilating elements,
199	  %  we see there exists an $f\ne 0$ in $A$ with $fM=0$. Hence $M_f =0$, proving
200	  %  the lemma when $n=-1$."
201	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (base case of the
202	  induction).}
203	  Suppose \(K \otimes_A M = 0\), where \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\). Then there exists
204	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(f M = 0\); consequently \(M_f = 0\) is free over
205	  \(A_f\).
206	\end{lemma}
207	
208	\begin{proof}
209	
210	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1719-L1726).
211	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (base case).}
212	  Since \(K \otimes_A M = S^{-1}M\) with \(S = A \setminus \{0\}\), the hypothesis
213	  \(M_K = 0\) means every element of \(M\) is killed by some non-zero element of
214	  \(A\). Choose a finite \(B\)-generating set \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) of \(M\) (which
215	  exists because \(M\) is a finite \(B\)-module), and for each \(i\) an
216	  \(a_i \in A\), \(a_i \ne 0\), with \(a_i v_i = 0\). The product
217	  \(f := a_1 \cdots a_k\) is non-zero (\(A\) is a domain) and annihilates every
218	  generator, hence \(f M = 0\). Therefore \(M_f = 0\), which is free over \(A_f\).
219	\end{proof}
220	
221	\begin{lemma}[L2 — prime-filtration induction principle]
222	  \label{lem:noeth_prime_filtration}
223	  \lean{IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime}
224	  \mathlibok
225	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
226	  Let \(B\) be a noetherian commutative ring and let \(P\) be a predicate on finite
227	  \(B\)-modules that (i) holds for every subsingleton module, (ii) holds for every
228	  cyclic quotient \(B/\mathfrak{p}\) with \(\mathfrak{p}\) prime, and (iii) is
229	  closed under short exact sequences (\(P(N_1)\) and \(P(N_3)\) imply \(P(N_2)\)
230	  for \(0 \to N_1 \to N_2 \to N_3 \to 0\)). Then \(P\) holds for every finite
231	  \(B\)-module. In particular every finite \(B\)-module \(M\) admits a finite
232	  filtration \(0 = M_0 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M\) with each quotient
233	  \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B/\mathfrak{p}_i\) for a prime \(\mathfrak{p}_i\), so to
234	  prove generic freeness for \(M\) it suffices to prove it for each
235	  \(B/\mathfrak{p}\) and to splice along the filtration's short exact sequences.
236	\end{lemma}

238	\begin{lemma}\leanok
239	[L3a — localisation of a short exact sequence is exact]
240	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact}
241	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact}
242	  Let \(0 \to M' \xrightarrow{i} M \xrightarrow{q} M'' \to 0\) be a short exact
243	  sequence of \(B\)-modules, and let \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\). Restricting scalars
244	  along \(A \to B\) and localising every term at the powers of \(f\) yields a short
245	  exact sequence
246	  \[
247	    0 \to M'_f \xrightarrow{i_f} M_f \xrightarrow{q_f} M''_f \to 0
248	  \]
249	  of \(A_f\)-modules; in particular the localised map \(i_f\) is injective and the
250	  localised map \(q_f\) is surjective. Here \(A_f = \texttt{Localization.Away}~f\)
251	  and \(M_f = \texttt{LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers}~f)\,M\), the localisation
252	  taken at the submonoid of powers of \(f\) after restricting scalars to \(A\).
253	\end{lemma}
254	
255	\begin{proof}
256	
257	  Localisation of modules at a multiplicative set is an exact functor: it preserves
258	  injections, surjections, and exactness at the middle term. Applying it to the
259	  scalar-restricted maps \(i\) and \(q\) (regarded as \(A\)-linear via the tower
260	  \(A \to B\)) returns the displayed sequence, exact in all three positions. In the
261	  formalisation this is the localisation-preserves-exactness API
262	  (\texttt{LocalizedModule.map\_exact}) applied to \(i\) and \(q\) regarded as
263	  \(A\)-linear maps via the scalar restriction along \(A \to B\).
264	\end{proof}
265	
266	\begin{lemma}\leanok
267	[L3b — free transport across a finer localisation]
268	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport}
269	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul}
270	  Let \(N\) be a \(B\)-module and \(f', f'' \in A\) with \(f = f' f''\). If the
271	  localisation \(N_{f'}\) is free over \(A_{f'}\), then the further localisation
272	  \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
273	\end{lemma}
274	
275	\begin{proof}
276	
277	  Because \(f = f' f''\), the localisation away from \(f\) factors through the
278	  localisation away from \(f'\): the ring \(A_f\) is the localisation of \(A_{f'}\)
279	  at the image of \(f''\), and likewise the module \(N_f\) is the localisation of
280	  \(N_{f'}\) at the image of \(f''\). A localisation of a free module is free over
281	  the localised base ring — a basis of \(N_{f'}\) over \(A_{f'}\) maps to a basis of
282	  \(N_f\) over \(A_f\). Hence \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Stated as a standalone
283	  fact, it is applied to both ends of the spliced sequence.
284	\end{proof}
285	
286	\begin{lemma}\leanok
287	[L3c — a short exact sequence with free ends has a free middle]
288	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
289	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_of_shortExact_of_free_free}
290	  Let \(0 \to M'_f \to M_f \to M''_f \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of
291	  \(A_f\)-modules in which both ends \(M'_f\) and \(M''_f\) are free over \(A_f\).
292	  Then \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\); concretely \(M_f \cong M'_f \oplus M''_f\).
293	\end{lemma}
294	
295	\begin{proof}
296	
297	  A free module is projective, so the free quotient \(M''_f\) is projective and the
298	  short exact sequence splits: \(M_f \cong M'_f \oplus M''_f\). A finite direct sum
299	  of free \(A_f\)-modules is free, hence \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
300	\end{proof}
301	
302	\begin{lemma}\leanok
303	[L3 — splicing fact for a short exact sequence (assembly)]
304	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact}
305	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact}
306	  \uses{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact,
307	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
308	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, splicing remark
309	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1737-L1745).
310	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
311	  % "Note that if $0\to M'\to M\to M'' \to 0$ is a short exact sequence of
312	  %  $B$-modules, and if $f'$ and $f''$ are non-zero elements of $A$ such that
313	  %  $M'_{f'}$ and $M''_{f''}$ are free over respectively $A_{f'}$ and $A_{f''}$,
314	  %  then $M_f$ is a free module over $A_f$ where $f=f'f''$. We will use this fact
315	  %  repeatedly. Therefore it is enough to prove the result when $M$ is of the form
316	  %  $B/\pp$ for a prime ideal $\pp$ in $B$. This reduces us to the case where $B$
317	  %  is a domain and $M=B$."
318	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (splicing fact).}
319	  Let \(0 \to M' \to M \to M'' \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of \(B\)-modules,
320	  and let \(f', f'' \in A\) be non-zero with \(M'_{f'}\) free over \(A_{f'}\) and
321	  \(M''_{f''}\) free over \(A_{f''}\). Then for \(f := f' f''\) the localisation
322	  \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
323	\end{lemma}
324	
325	\begin{proof}
326	  \uses{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact,
327	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
328	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1737-L1745).
329	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (splicing fact).}
330	  Set \(f := f' f''\); since \(A\) is a domain and \(f', f'' \ne 0\), the product is
331	  non-zero, so \(f \ne 0\). Localising the given short exact sequence at the powers
332	  of \(f\) (after restricting scalars to \(A\)) produces a short exact sequence
333	  \(0 \to M'_f \to M_f \to M''_f \to 0\) of \(A_f\)-modules, by
334	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact}. Both ends are free over \(A_f\):
335	  applying \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport} to \(M'\) with the
336	  factorisation \(f = f' f''\) (using that \(M'_{f'}\) is free over \(A_{f'}\)) shows
337	  \(M'_f\) is free over \(A_f\), and applying the same lemma to \(M''\) with the
338	  factorisation \(f = f'' f'\) (using that \(M''_{f''}\) is free over \(A_{f''}\))
339	  shows \(M''_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Finally
340	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split} concludes that the middle term \(M_f\) is
341	  free over \(A_f\).
342	\end{proof}

344	\begin{lemma}[L4 anchor — Noether normalisation over a field]
345	  \label{lem:noether_normalization_fg}

349	  Let \(k\) be a field and \(R\) a non-trivial finite-type \(k\)-algebra. Then there

355	\begin{lemma}

358	[L4a — clearing one equation's denominators]
359	  \label{lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
360	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_clear_one_denominator}
361	  % NOTE (shared engine): the denominator-clearing performed here -- pushing finitely
362	  % many coefficients of one polynomial from K = Frac(A) down into a single
363	  % Localization.Away g -- is the same primitive the torsion reindex (L5b,
364	  % lem:gf_torsion_reindex) uses when it inverts the leading coefficient of the
365	  % Nagata-normalised annihilator. A prover who writes one general "clear finitely many
366	  % denominators into one Localization.Away g" helper can have both lemmas consume it.
367	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
368	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1755-L1759).
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
370	  % "If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for
371	  %  coefficients of equations of integral dependence
372	  %  satisfied by a finite set of
373	  %  algebra generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$,
374	  %  we see that $B_g$ is finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
375	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
376	  %   Let K := FractionRing A and A_g := Localization.Away g; the canonical map
377	  %   A_g -> K (induced because powers of g are non-zero-divisors of A) extends along
378	  %   MvPolynomial.map to MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g -> MvPolynomial (Fin n) K.
379	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
380	  %       (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
381	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0),
382	  %         p ∈ Set.range
383	  %           (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)))
384	  % NOTE (iter-008 encoding): there is NO canonical `Algebra (Localization.Away g)
385	  %   (FractionRing A)` instance, so the landed Lean does not use the literal
386	  %   `algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)` above. It encodes the same map
387	  %   as `IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle` with
388	  %   `hle : powers g ≤ comap id (nonZeroDivisors A)` — mathematically identical, just the
389	  %   well-typed form. The proof is axiom-clean.
390	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (common denominator).}
391	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\), and let
392	  \(p \in K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) be a polynomial with coefficients in \(K\) (for instance,
393	  one coefficient polynomial of an equation of integral dependence over
394	  \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)). Then there exists \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), such that \(p\)
395	  lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
396	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\); equivalently, \(p\) has all of its
397	  coefficients in \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\).
398	\end{lemma}
399	
400	\begin{proof}
401	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1755-L1759).
402	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (common denominator).}
403	  The polynomial \(p\) has finitely many non-zero coefficients
404	  \(c_1, \dots, c_r \in K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\). Each \(c_j\) is a fraction
405	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) with \(a_j \in A\) and \(s_j \in A \setminus \{0\}\). Put
406	  \(g := s_1 \cdots s_r \in A\); since \(A\) is a domain and each \(s_j \ne 0\), also
407	  \(g \ne 0\). For each \(j\) the denominator \(s_j\) divides \(g\), so
408	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) lies in the subring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\). Hence every
409	  coefficient of \(p\) lies in \(A_g\), and \(p\) is the coefficient-extension of a
410	  polynomial in \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
411	\end{proof}

414	[L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators]
415	  \label{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators}
416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial}
417	  \uses{lem:noether_normalization_fg, lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
418	  % NOTE (resolved iter-006): the LEAN SIGNATURE block below now matches the landed
419	  % Lean decl `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`, which RETAINS the binder
420	  % `(_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))` as
421	  % the 3rd existential (after `(_ : g ≠ 0)`, before `φ`) — required to type the
422	  % AlgHom target as an A_g-algebra. The MATH statement is unaffected.
423	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (landed target, AlgHom encoding). Write
424	  %   A_g := Localization.Away g,   B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g).
425	  % The conclusion is:
426	  %   ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
427	  %     (_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
428	  %     (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)
429	  %           →ₐ[Localization.Away g] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
430	  %     Function.Injective φ ∧
431	  %     (letI := φ.toAlgebra;
432	  %      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g))
433	  %                    (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
434	  % The polynomial subalgebra A_g[b_1,…,b_n] is realised as the DOMAIN
435	  % `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`, embedded into B_g by the algebra map φ; the
436	  % generators b_j are φ(X_j). Compared with an earlier draft using three anonymous
437	  % instance existentials (∃ (_ : Algebra A_g B_g) (_ : Algebra (MvPoly … A_g) B_g)
438	  % (_ : IsScalarTower …)), this form keeps only the single `Algebra A_g B_g` binder
439	  % needed to type B_g as an A_g-algebra: the single φ then carries the algebra map,
440	  % `Function.Injective φ` encodes algebraic independence of the b_j, and
441	  % `Module.Finite … over φ.toAlgebra` encodes module-finiteness of B_g over the
442	  % image polynomial subalgebra. Downstream, L5 (\cref{lem:gf_polynomial_core})
443	  % consumes a finite module over `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`; the AlgHom hands it
444	  % exactly that algebra structure via `φ.toAlgebra`, with no instance revival.
445	  % Why B_g and not B: generic flatness only needs the property after one
446	  % inversion g; the integral-dependence equations only clear denominators after
447	  % inverting g (see the SOURCE QUOTE PROOF below).
448	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
449	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1747-L1758).
450	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
451	  % "By Noether normalisation lemma, there exist elements $b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B$,
452	  %  such that $K\otimes_AB$ is finite over its subalgebra $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$ and
453	  %  the elements $b_1,\ldots, b_n$ are algebraically independent over $K$. [...]
454	  %  If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for coefficients of
455	  %  equations of integral dependence satisfied by a finite set of algebra
456	  %  generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$, we see that $B_g$ is
457	  %  finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
458	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (Noether
459	  normalisation with denominators).}
460	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K\) and \(B\) a finite-type
461	  \(A\)-algebra that is a domain with \(B_K = K \otimes_A B \ne 0\). Write
462	  \(A_g = \texttt{Localization.Away}~g\) and
463	  \(B_g = \texttt{Localization.Away (algebraMap } A\,B\,g)\) for the localisations
464	  away from \(g\) and its image. Then there exist a natural number \(n\), an element
465	  \(g \in A\) with \(g \ne 0\), and an injective \(A_g\)-algebra homomorphism
466	  \[
467	    \varphi : A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \longrightarrow B_g,
468	  \]
469	  where \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] = \texttt{MvPolynomial (Fin } n)~A_g\) is the
470	  polynomial ring on \(n\) algebraically independent variables, such that \(B_g\) is
471	  module-finite over the image polynomial subalgebra via \(\varphi\). The injectivity
472	  of \(\varphi\) records the algebraic independence of the generators
473	  \(b_j := \varphi(X_j)\), and the module-finiteness is taken over the algebra
474	  structure that \(\varphi\) puts on \(B_g\). Equivalently, the classical statement
475	  ``\(B_g\) is finite over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)'' is encoded by the single map
476	  \(\varphi\): it both names the polynomial subalgebra (as the domain
477	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\)) and carries the algebra structure under which \(B_g\) is
478	  finite.
479	\end{lemma}
480	
481	\begin{proof}
482	  \uses{lem:noether_normalization_fg, lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
483	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1747-L1758).
484	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (Noether normalisation with denominators).}
485	  % NOTE (iter-007 decomposition): the denominator-clearing middle of this proof is a
486	  % Finset-fold of lem:gf_clear_one_denominator over the finite generating set. Each
487	  % generator satisfies one equation of integral dependence whose coefficient
488	  % polynomials lie in K[b_1,...,b_n]; gf_clear_one_denominator clears the denominators
489	  % of one such coefficient polynomial into a single Localization.Away g_i. Folding over
490	  % the finite set of (generator, coefficient) pairs and multiplying the finitely many
491	  % g_i yields one common g != 0 over which all integrality relations hold; the AlgHom
492	  % assembly below then runs over A_g exactly as before.
493	
494	  \emph{Step 1 — Noether normalisation over \(K\).} Apply Noether normalisation over the
495	  field \(K\) (\cref{lem:noether_normalization_fg}) to the finite-type \(K\)-algebra
496	  \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\): there are algebraically independent
497	  \(\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n \in B_K\) with \(B_K\) module-finite over
498	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). Clearing denominators, each \(\bar b_j\) is
499	  represented by some \(b_j \in B\) (writing \(1 \otimes b\) as \(b\)).
500	
501	  \emph{Step 2 — clear the integral-dependence denominators to a single \(g\).} Fix a
502	  finite set of algebra generators of \(B_K\) over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\); each
503	  generator satisfies an equation of integral dependence whose coefficients are
504	  polynomials in \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\), and there are finitely many such
505	  coefficient polynomials in total. Applying \cref{lem:gf_clear_one_denominator} to each
506	  coefficient polynomial yields a non-zero element of \(A\) over which that polynomial's
507	  coefficients become integral; folding over the finite collection (multiplying the
508	  finitely many elements) produces a single \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), such that
509	  \emph{every} coefficient of \emph{every} integral-dependence equation lies in
510	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). After inverting \(g\) all these integrality relations hold
511	  over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), and the chosen generators generate \(B_g\) as a module
512	  over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); hence \(B_g\) is module-finite over
513	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\).
514	
515	  \emph{Step 3 — AlgHom assembly.} The algebra homomorphism \(\varphi\) of the statement
516	  is the \(A_g\)-algebra map \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to B_g\) sending
517	  \(X_j \mapsto b_j\); the algebraic independence of the \(b_j\) over \(A_g\) (inherited
518	  from their independence over \(K\)) makes \(\varphi\) injective, and the
519	  module-finiteness just established is exactly module-finiteness of \(B_g\) over the
520	  image of \(\varphi\).
521	\end{proof}

523	\begin{lemma}
524	
525	\leanok
526	[L5a — the generic-rank short exact sequence]
527	  \label{lem:gf_generic_rank_ses}
528	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_generic_rank_ses}
529	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step

531	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
532	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the
533	  %  domain $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact
534	  %  sequence of $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
535	  %  $$0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$$
536	  %  where $T$ is a finite torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
537	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
538	  %   Write P_d := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A.
539	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
540	  %       (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
541	  %       [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
542	  %       [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
543	  %       [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
544	  %       ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
545	  %                        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
546	  %         Function.Injective φ ∧
547	  %         Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)
548	  %   witness: m = Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d)
549	  %                  (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N), the generic rank;
550	  %            φ = Fintype.linearCombination P_d v, with v : Fin m → N the
551	  %            denominator-cleared lifts of a FractionRing P_d-basis of the localisation.
552	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (generic-rank SES).}
553	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(d \ge 0\), and \(N\) a finite module over the
554	  polynomial ring \(P_d := A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) (with its induced \(A\)-module structure
555	  through the scalar tower \(A \to P_d \to N\)). Then there exist \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) and
556	  an injective \(P_d\)-linear map \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) whose cokernel
557	  \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\) is a torsion \(P_d\)-module. Equivalently, there is
558	  a short exact sequence of \(P_d\)-modules
559	  \[
560	    0 \to P_d^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } N \to T \to 0,
561	    \qquad T \text{ torsion}.
562	  \]
563	
564	  The integer \(m\) is the \emph{generic rank} of \(N\): the dimension of the
565	  \(\mathrm{Frac}(P_d)\)-vector space
566	  \(\mathrm{Frac}(P_d) \otimes_{P_d} N\), i.e.\ of the localisation of \(N\) at the
567	  non-zero-divisors of the domain \(P_d\). This step lives over \(P_d\) directly —
568	  \emph{no inversion of any element \(g \in A\) is required here}; the element \(g\) of
569	  Nitsure's argument enters only at the reindexing step \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex}.
570	\end{lemma}
571	
572	\begin{proof}
573	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1765).
574	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (generic-rank SES).}
575	  Write \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\); since \(A\) is a noetherian domain, so is \(P_d\)
576	  (Hilbert basis theorem, and a polynomial ring over a domain is a domain). Let
577	  \(Q = \mathrm{Frac}(P_d)\) be its fraction field and
578	  \(N_Q := Q \otimes_{P_d} N\) the localisation of \(N\) at the non-zero-divisors of
579	  \(P_d\); as \(N\) is finite over \(P_d\), \(N_Q\) is a finite-dimensional
580	  \(Q\)-vector space. Put \(m := \dim_Q N_Q\), the generic rank.
581	
582	  Choose a \(Q\)-basis of \(N_Q\) and clear denominators: each basis vector is a
583	  \(Q\)-unit multiple of the image of some \(v_i \in N\), so the images of
584	  \(v_1, \dots, v_m \in N\) again form a \(Q\)-basis of \(N_Q\). In particular the family
585	  \((v_i)_{i}\) is linearly independent over \(Q\); restricting scalars along the
586	  injection \(P_d \hookrightarrow Q\), it is linearly independent over \(P_d\). Let
587	  \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) be the \(P_d\)-linear map
588	  \((c_1, \dots, c_m) \mapsto \sum_i c_i v_i\). Linear independence of \((v_i)\) over
589	  \(P_d\) makes \(\varphi\) injective.
590	
591	  Set \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\). Because the images of \(v_1, \dots, v_m\)
592	  already span \(N_Q\) over \(Q\), the localised map \(\varphi \otimes Q : Q^{\oplus m}
593	  \to N_Q\) is an isomorphism, so \(Q \otimes_{P_d} T = 0\). A finite \(P_d\)-module whose
594	  localisation at the non-zero-divisors of \(P_d\) vanishes is torsion; hence \(T\) is a
595	  torsion \(P_d\)-module. This is the asserted short exact sequence.
596	\end{proof}

640	  %   left to the assembly (a surjection P_d ↠ P_d/(F) pushes a finite generating set).
641	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
642	  %       (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]

648	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (torsion annihilation).}
649	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(d \ge 0\), and \(T\) a finite \emph{torsion} module
650	  over \(P_d := A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\). Then there exists a non-zero
651	  \(F \in \operatorname{Ann}_{P_d}(T)\); consequently the \(P_d\)-action on \(T\) factors
652	  through \(P_d/(F)\) and \(T\) is a finite module over \(P_d/(F)\).

654	
655	\begin{proof}
656	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1724-L1727).
657	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (torsion annihilation).}
658	  The ring \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) is a noetherian domain (a polynomial ring over the
659	  noetherian domain \(A\)), and \(T\) is a finite torsion \(P_d\)-module. By
660	  \cref{lem:annihilator_meets_nonZeroDivisors} the annihilator of \(T\) meets the
661	  non-zero-divisors of \(P_d\); choose \(F\) in that intersection. In the domain \(P_d\) a
662	  non-zero-divisor is exactly a non-zero element, so \(F \ne 0\), and \(F \cdot T = 0\).
663	  Since \(F\) annihilates \(T\), the \(P_d\)-module structure descends along the quotient
664	  map \(P_d \twoheadrightarrow P_d/(F)\); the image of any finite \(P_d\)-generating set of
665	  \(T\) generates \(T\) over \(P_d/(F)\), so \(T\) is finite over \(P_d/(F)\).
666	\end{proof}

670	  \label{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}
671	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}

673	  %   MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv, which singles out variable X_0 as the polynomial
674	  %   variable over the coefficient ring MvPolynomial (Fin n) A. The choice of which of

677	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
678	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1766-L1768);
679	  % the Nagata substitution is the standard device realising the support-dimension drop.
680	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
681	  % "Therefore, the dimension of the support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$
682	  %  as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less than $n$. Hence by
683	  %  induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),"
684	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
685	  %   A_g := Localization.Away g; map := MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A A_g).
686	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
687	  %       (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
688	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
689	  %         (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) A),
690	  %         IsUnit
691	  %           (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) n
692	  %             (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff
693	  %   The automorphism e is the triangular Nagata substitution; g is the (non-zero)
694	  %   leading coefficient that becomes a unit after inversion.
695	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (support-dimension drop).}
696	  Let \(A\) be a domain, \(n \ge 0\), and \(0 \ne F \in A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\). Then there
697	  exist a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) and a
698	  non-zero \(g \in A\) such that, after inverting \(g\), the image of \(e(F)\) in
699	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) — viewed via \(\texttt{finSuccEquiv}\) as a univariate polynomial
700	  in \(X_0\) over \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) — has a \emph{unit} leading coefficient (namely
701	  the image of \(g\)). Equivalently, \(e(F)\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\) over
702	  \(A_g\).
703	\end{lemma}
704	
705	\begin{proof}
706	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
707	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop, Nagata normalisation).}
708	  Apply the Nagata substitution \(X_i \mapsto X_i + X_0^{r^{\,i}}\) for \(i = 1, \dots, n\)
709	  and \(X_0 \mapsto X_0\), with \(r\) larger than every exponent occurring in \(F\): it is a
710	  triangular (hence invertible) \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\).
711	  Under this substitution the distinct monomials of \(F\) acquire distinct degrees in
712	  \(X_0\), so \(e(F)\), expanded as a polynomial in \(X_0\), has a unique top-degree term
713	  whose coefficient is the coefficient \(c \in A\) of the top total-degree monomial of
714	  \(F\); since \(F \ne 0\) this \(c \ne 0\). Put \(g := c\). The degree of \(e(F)\) in
715	  \(X_0\) equals the total degree of \(F\), and its leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is the
716	  constant \(g\). Passing to \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\), the image of \(g\) is a unit, so the
717	  image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\).
718	\end{proof}

41	### Decision 1: how to express the generic rank `m`
42	
43	- **Mathlib idiom**: `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)`.
44	  `P_d = MvPolynomial (Fin d) A` is a noetherian domain (`MvPolynomial.isDomain`, Hilbert basis), so
45	  `nonZeroDivisors P_d` localizes `N` to a module over `Localization (nonZeroDivisors P_d) = FractionRing P_d`,
46	  a finite-dimensional `FractionRing P_d`-vector space (finite, since `N` is finite over `P_d`).
47	  `Module.finrank` over a field is the right invariant; `Module.rank` (`Cardinal`-valued) is the wrong
48	  altitude — you want a `ℕ` to index `Fin m`.
49	- **Choosing `m` lifts of `N` whose images form a basis** — atoms all verified:
50	  - `Module.finBasis (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule … N) : Basis (Fin m) (FractionRing P_d) (…)`
51	    (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Free`) — the field-vector-space basis indexed by `Fin (finrank)`.
52	  - `IsLocalizedModule.surj (nonZeroDivisors P_d) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap …)` — every basis vector
53	    is `(1/s) • (image of some n ∈ N)`; `s` a unit in the field, so the **images** `{mk n_i}` are again a
54	    basis (clear denominators). (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`.)
55	  - `LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars` (P_d ↪ FractionRing P_d injective) descends linear independence
56	    of the lifts from `FractionRing P_d` back to `P_d` — this is what makes `φ` (below) injective.
57	  - Reverse helper `Module.Basis.ofIsLocalizedModule` / `LinearIndependent.localization`
58	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) go R→Rₛ; useful for the spanning/surjectivity half.
59	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent — Mathlib has every atom but **no single packaged "choose `m` elements
60	  whose images are a basis of the localization" lemma**. The number is idiomatic; the lift is project glue.
61	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the number (`Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule …)`),
62	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL on the lift (small, all anchors present).
63	
64	### Decision 2: encoding the generic-rank SES `gf_generic_rank_ses`
65	
66	- **Mathlib idiom for the map**: `φ := Fintype.linearCombination P_d v` where `v : Fin m → N` are the
67	  lifts of Decision 1 (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Finsupp.LinearCombination`). Domain `(Fin m → P_d)` **is**
68	  the concrete `P_d^{⊕m}` (matches the `M'` slot of L3 exactly). Companions: `Fintype.linearCombination_apply`
69	  (`∑ i, f i • v i`), `Fintype.range_linearCombination` (`range = span (Set.range v)`).
70	  `φ` injective ⟺ `LinearIndependent P_d v` (got from Decision 1 via `restrict_scalars`).
71	- **Cokernel / torsion**: `T := N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ`; the surjection is `Submodule.mkQ (range φ)`
72	  (surjective, and `Function.Exact φ (mkQ (range φ))` holds — `range φ = ker (mkQ)`). `T` torsion encoded
73	  as `Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T` (preferred — it is the literal input of the reindex's
74	  annihilator anchor) or equivalently `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) T)` via
75	  `LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff`. Torsion holds because the images of `v` span the localization
76	  (basis), so `φ ⊗ FractionRing P_d` is surjective, hence `T ⊗ FractionRing P_d = 0`.
77	- **Important simplification vs the directive**: the SES is constructible over `P_d` **directly — no
78	  inversion of `g ∈ A` is needed for this step**. `φ` is injective on the nose (`ker φ` is torsion in the
79	  torsion-free `P_d`-module `P_d^{⊕m}`, hence `0`), and `T` is torsion on the nose. The `g` that Nitsure
80	  mentions belongs entirely to the *reindex* (Decision 3), not the SES. So `gf_generic_rank_ses` carries
81	  no `g`. (The "denominator-clearing exactness after inverting one element" API the directive asked about
82	  is therefore unnecessary here.)
83	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — no packaged generic-rank SES, but every atom exists and the assembly
84	  is short.
85	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (build `gf_generic_rank_ses` from the atoms above).

87	### Decision 3: the torsion reindex `gf_torsion_reindex` — and the base-domain induction shape
88	
89	- **Nonzero annihilator (Mathlib ✓)**: `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
90	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Torsion.Basic`): a finite `Module.IsTorsion R M` over comm `R` has
91	  `(⊤.annihilator ∩ nonZeroDivisors R).Nonempty`. For the domain `P_d` this gives `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`.
92	- **Variable drop (Mathlib-ABSENT)**: from `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)`, one wants `T` module-finite over a polynomial
93	  ring in `m' < d` variables. Cleanest route that **avoids Krull-dimension theory**: a Nagata change of
94	  variables makes `F` monic in `X_d` up to its leading coefficient `g ∈ A`; after inverting `g`,
95	  `A_g[X_1..X_d]/(F)` is module-finite (division algorithm) over `A_g[X_1..X_{d-1}] = MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) A_g`,
96	  and `T` (finite over `P_d/(F)`) is finite over it by `Module.Finite.trans`. So `m' = d-1 < d` — **no
97	  `ringKrullDim` needed**. Mathlib has the *field* Noether normalization `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`
98	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) and `ringKrullDim` theory (`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.*`,
99	  e.g. `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_le`) but **not** the domain-level single-variable elimination with
100	  denominator clearing. This is the **same engine** the already-stubbed L4
101	  `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial` needs — build once, reuse.
102	- **CRITICAL design consequence — the induction must generalize the base domain `A`.** The reindex inverts
103	  `g ∈ A`, so `T_g` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)` — base ring `A_g`, **not**
104	  `A`. The IH of `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` as currently written fixes `A`, so
105	  `IH m' _ T_g` does **not typecheck** (wrong base ring). This is the real cause of the iter-006
106	  "signatures depend on the generic-rank API" stall: it is not the rank API, it is that the recursion
107	  changes the base. **Fix**: state/prove the core with the motive universally quantifying the noetherian
108	  domain — `∀ d, ∀ (A) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (N) […], ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Free A_f N_f`,
109	  by `Nat.strong_induction_on d` with `A` (and its three instances, and all of `N`'s `d`-dependent
110	  instances) reverted into the motive. `A` is already an explicit argument, so this is a reversion change,
111	  not a signature change to the public lemma. Apply `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) (Localization.Away g) T_g` at
112	  base `A_g` (still a noetherian domain: `IsLocalization.isDomain`/`Localization.Away` of a noeth domain).
113	- **Descent of the IH witness back to `A`**: IH gives `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `T_g` free over `(A_g)_h`.
114	  Since `A_g = Localization.Away g` and `h = a / g^k`, `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` and `T_{g·a} ≅ (T_g)_h`, so the
115	  freeness descends to `f'' := g·a ∈ A`. This is the same localization-tower freeness transport as the
116	  already-proved L3b `free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul` (a thin bridge across `A → A_g → (A_g)_h`).
117	- **The `P_d^{⊕m}` end is free over `A_f` for free (Mathlib ✓)**: `MvPolynomial.instFree`
118	  (`Module.Free A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)`) + `Pi`/localization free, so the `M'` end of L3 is free over
119	  `A_{f'}` for *any* `f' ≠ 0`. (Note: the file's comment "applied coordinatewise via the d=0 leaf" is
120	  slightly off — `P_d^{⊕m}` is **not** module-finite over `A`, but it **is** free, which is all L3 needs.)
121	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (variable-drop engine + base-`A` generalization).
122	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.

124	## Recommended sub-lemma signatures (for the blueprint pin)
125	
126	```lean
127	/-- `gf_generic_rank_ses` — the generic-rank short exact sequence. No `g`: built over `P_d` directly. -/
128	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
129	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
130	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
131	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
132	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
133	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
134	      Function.Injective φ ∧
135	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)
136	-- witness: m = finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N);
137	--          φ = Fintype.linearCombination P_d v, v the denominator-cleared basis lifts.
138	```
139	
140	```lean
141	/-- `gf_torsion_reindex` — re-present a finite torsion `P_d`-module over fewer variables after
142	inverting `g ∈ A`. Mirrors the existential style of the already-present L4
143	`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`. -/
144	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
145	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
146	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
147	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
148	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
149	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
150	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
151	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
152	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
153	      (_ : Module (Localization.Away g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
154	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
155	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
156	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
157	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
158	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)
159	-- can fix m' = d - 1 (single-variable elimination); strong induction accepts any m' < d.
160	```
161	
162	And the **core's proof must be restructured** to generalize `A`:
163	```lean
164	-- helper, then `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` is its specialization:
165	∀ d, ∀ (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
166	       (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
167	       [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module A N]
168	       [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N],
169	     ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N)
170	-- proved by `Nat.strong_induction_on d`, A reverted into the motive.
171	```

173	## Recommendation
174	Pin `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)` as the generic
175	rank — idiomatic and `ℕ`-valued. Build `gf_generic_rank_ses` (no `g`) from `Fintype.linearCombination`
176	on denominator-cleared basis lifts (`Module.finBasis` + `IsLocalizedModule.surj` +
177	`LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars`), with `T := N ⧸ range φ` and `Module.IsTorsion P_d T`. Build
178	`gf_torsion_reindex` on the L4 existential template, using `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
179	for `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)` and the **project-side** Nagata single-variable elimination + denominator clearing
180	(shared with L4) to land over `MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) (Localization.Away g)`. **The load-bearing fix is
181	structural**: generalize the base domain `A` in the `Nat.strong_induction_on`, because the reindex changes
182	the base ring to `A_g` and the IH must be available there. None of these require weakening any pinned type.
183

739	  % NOTE (shared engine): this is the standalone division-algorithm primitive that both
740	  % L5b (lem:gf_torsion_reindex, after inverting the Nagata leading coefficient) and L4
741	  % (lem:gf_clear_one_denominator / lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators, after clearing
742	  % denominators) consume. It is stated over a GENERAL base ring R so it is not tied to
743	  % the torsion module T; build it once in Lean and reuse for both. The hypothesis
744	  % "leading coefficient is a unit" (monic up to a unit) is what the inversion step in
745	  % each caller produces; the strict-monic Mathlib anchor is reached inside the proof by
746	  % rescaling by the unit.
747	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
748	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1766-L1768);
749	  % the division algorithm in one variable is the standard mechanism of the support drop.
750	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
751	  % "Therefore, the dimension of the support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$
752	  %  as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less than $n$. Hence by
753	  %  induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),"
754	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (matches the landed decl verbatim).
755	  %   Finiteness is the `RingHom.Finite` of the composite ring map
756	  %   R[X_1,…,X_n] ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n] ↠ R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)
757	  %   (constant inclusion X_j ↦ X_{j+1} = MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ, then
758	  %   Ideal.Quotient.mk). A caller discharges a `RingHom.Finite` goal, not `Module.Finite`.
759	  %     (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
760	  %       (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
761	  %       (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
762	  %       ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
763	  %           (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
764	  %             MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite
765	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (single-variable elimination).}
766	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(n \in \mathbb{N}\), and \(p \in R[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) a
767	  polynomial whose image under the isomorphism
768	  \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n] \cong (R[X_1, \dots, X_n])[X_0]\) (\(\texttt{finSuccEquiv}\)) has a
769	  \emph{unit} leading coefficient — i.e.\ \(p\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to a unit. Then the
770	  quotient \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p)\) is module-finite over the coefficient subring
771	  \(R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) (embedded by \(X_j \mapsto X_j\), \(j = 1, \dots, n\)).
772	\end{lemma}
773	
774	\begin{proof}
775	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
776	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (single-variable elimination).}
777	  Write \(S := R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) and let
778	  \(\Phi : R[X_0, \dots, X_n] \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } S[X_0]\) be the \(R\)-algebra
779	  isomorphism \(\texttt{finSuccEquiv}\), which sends \(X_0\) to the univariate variable and
780	  embeds \(S\) as the constants. Put \(q := \Phi(p) \in S[X_0]\); by hypothesis its leading
781	  coefficient \(u\) is a unit, so the associate \(\tilde q := u^{-1} \cdot q\) is monic and
782	  \((\tilde q) = (q)\) in \(S[X_0]\). The isomorphism \(\Phi\) carries the ideal \((p)\) onto
783	  \((q) = (\tilde q)\), hence descends to a ring isomorphism
784	  \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p) \cong S[X_0]/(\tilde q)\) that is \(S\)-linear, because \(S\) sits
785	  inside both sides as the constants (respectively as the coefficient subring
786	  \(R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\)). By \cref{lem:polynomial_monic_quotient_finite} the quotient
787	  \(S[X_0]/(\tilde q)\) is module-finite over \(S\); transporting finiteness along the
788	  \(S\)-linear isomorphism shows \(R[X_0, \dots, X_n]/(p)\) is module-finite over
789	  \(S = R[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
790	\end{proof}

796	  \label{lem:gf_torsion_reindex}

800	  % NOTE (shared engine): the single-variable Nagata change-of-variables together with

803	  % lem:gf_clear_one_denominator) requires. It should be built once in Lean and reused by
804	  % both: a general "make a nonzero F monic in the last variable and clear the leading

806	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
807	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1766-L1768).
808	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:

811	  %  induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),"
812	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
813	  %   Write A_g := Localization.Away g and T_g := LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T.
814	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
815	  %       (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
816	  %       [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]

819	  %       (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
820	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
821	  %         (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
822	  %                 (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
823	  %         (_ : Module (Localization.Away g)
824	  %                 (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))

826	  %                 (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
827	  %                 (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),

830	  %   one may always take m' = d - 1 (single-variable elimination); the strong induction
831	  %   accepts any m' < d.
832	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (support-dimension drop).}
833	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(d \ge 1\), and \(T\) a finite \emph{torsion} module
834	  over \(P_d := A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) (with its induced \(A\)-module structure). Then there
835	  exist a non-zero \(g \in A\) and an integer \(m' < d\) such that, after inverting \(g\),
836	  the localised module \(T_g := T[g^{-1}]\) is module-finite over the polynomial ring
837	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) in \(m'\) variables over the base ring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\);
838	  that is, \(T_g\) carries compatible \(A_g\)- and \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)-module
839	  structures (via the scalar tower \(A_g \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)) and is finite over
840	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\). One may always take \(m' = d - 1\).
841	\end{lemma}
842	
843	\begin{proof}
844	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
845	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop).}
846	  \uses{lem:gf_torsion_annihilator, lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar,
847	        lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic}
848	  Write \(d = (d-1) + 1\) and \(P_d = A[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\) (the distinguished variable
849	  is \(X_0\), the one eliminated by \(\texttt{finSuccEquiv}\); cf.\ the variable-convention
850	  note on \cref{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar}).
851	
852	  \emph{Annihilator.} By \cref{lem:gf_torsion_annihilator} there is a non-zero
853	  \(F \in \operatorname{Ann}_{P_d}(T)\), and \(T\) is a finite module over \(P_d/(F)\).
854	
855	  \emph{Nagata normalisation.} Applying \cref{lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar} to \(F \ne 0\)
856	  yields a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(P_d\) and a non-zero
857	  \(g \in A\) such that the image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\) has a unit
858	  leading coefficient in \(X_0\). The automorphism \(e\) induces a ring isomorphism
859	  \(P_d/(F) \cong P_d/(e\,F)\), so \(T\) is also finite over \(P_d/(e\,F)\). Localising at
860	  the powers of \(g\) (localisation commutes with the quotient), \(T_g\) is finite over
861	  \(P_d[g^{-1}]/(e\,F) = A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\).
862	
863	  \emph{Elimination.} Apply the shared engine
864	  \cref{lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic} with \(R = A_g\) and \(p\) the image of
865	  \(e(F)\) (whose \(\texttt{finSuccEquiv}\)-leading coefficient is a unit): the quotient
866	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\) is module-finite over the coefficient subring
867	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{d-1}]\), a polynomial ring in \(m' := d - 1\) variables over
868	  \(A_g\).
869	
870	  \emph{Transitivity.} Composing the two module-finite extensions, \(T_g\) is finite over
871	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\). Since \(m' = d - 1 < d\) (using \(d \ge 1\)), this is the
872	  required reindexing; no Krull-dimension theory is needed, the number of variables
873	  dropping by exactly one through the single-variable elimination.
874	\end{proof}
